title: Misuse of Abstract Class: Abstract Class Should Have Both Abstract and Concrete Methods
description:
The purpose of an abstract class is to provide some heritable behaviors while also defining methods which must be implemented by sub-classes.
A class with no abstract methods that was made abstract purely to prevent instantiation should be converted to a concrete class (i.e. remove the abstract keyword) with a private constructor.
A class with only abstract methods and no inheritable behavior should be converted to an interface.
recommendation: Convert abstract classes with no abstract methods to concrete classes or interfaces as appropriate.

checks.message: An abstract class must not be declared without abstract methods; consider converting to a concrete class with a private constructor.
An abstract class with no abstract methods should not exist; it should be a concrete class.
An abstract class with only abstract methods should be an interface.
